# Sequences as Data

## Draft focus

Show how DNA, RNA, and protein sequences can be represented, transformed, and annotated in Python.

## Starter example

```{python}
complement_map = str.maketrans("ATGC", "TACG")
sequence = "ATGCCGTA"
reverse_complement = sequence.translate(complement_map)[::-1]
reverse_complement
```

## Draft note

Later expand this chapter with Biopython and feature annotations.
